forecasts.reasoning: Without real-time search capabilities up to April 29, 2025, I must rely on historical patterns and prior events. Alassane Ouattara would be 83 in 2025. While his party (RHDP) has consistently pushed for his candidacy, he has previously expressed a desire to retire and hand over to a younger generation, though he tied this to his rivals (Gbagbo, Bédié) also stepping down. With Bédié's passing but Gbagbo remaining active, Ouattara's decision is contentious. Furthermore, even if he decides to run, African incumbents frequently delay their formal declarations until the last few months before an election (often July or August for an October election) to project focus on state duties or orchestrate a 'draft' by their party. Thus, there's a strong possibility that either 1) he chooses a successor instead of running himself, or 2) he runs but delays his announcement until after June 1. Taking into account the chances that he might have already declared earlier in 2024/2025 and the chance of a May declaration, I estimate the probability at 42%.
